ground_truth.title: How to Book a Flight: 11 Steps (with Pictures) - wikiHow
ground_truth.author: Shelby Reyes
ground_truth.main_content:
How to Book a Flight: A Comprehensive Guide

Booking a flight is one of the most important parts of finalizing your travel plans. Between airlines constantly changing prices and the many options of where to purchase your flight, the process can get confusing. This guide covers two main methods for booking flights: online booking and booking with an airline or travel agent.

Best Tips for Booking a Flight

Family travel expert Shelby Reyes recommends using Google Flights to check out typical ticket prices for when you want to travel and setting up alerts to find out when prices change. Outline your travel plans beforehand to get tickets that fit your schedule, then compare flight times, prices, and what they offer.

Method 1: Booking a Flight Online

Step 1: Outline your tentative travel plans. Think about where you plan or might like to travel, the dates you would like to go, and if you just want to book flights or a package deal. Make a list of your plans and have them handy and easily accessible while you book.

Step 2: Consider being flexible in your plans. The more flexible you are on everything from departure and arrival airlines and airports to travel dates and package deals, the more likely you are to get a great deal on your flight. Wednesdays are generally the cheapest day to travel. You can often find good deals on last minute flights, especially if you buy in conjunction with a hotel and/or rental car. Flying from alternative airports can often be cheaper and offer better connection times than large airport hubs.

Step 3: Compare flight prices. How much a flight costs varies greatly depending on many variables including the day you book, how far in advance you book, and even the website on which you book. By comparing prices from different sites, you are likely to get the best flight deal. Book approximately six weeks in advance if you can for the best flight options and prices. Tuesdays around 3pm Eastern time are the cheapest time to book your flight. Travel sites like Kayak, Expedia, CheapTickets, and Priceline collate information on the best flight prices and available times. Airline websites are also an excellent place to book tickets.

Step 4: Keep a list of flight fares and offers. As you compare flight offers, keep a list of all relevant details including departure and arrival airports and times, fees, and cancellation policies. Note if the price includes such items as taxes and baggage fees. Read each flight's cancellation policies and change fees.

Step 5: Purchase your ticket. Once you decide on the right flight for your upcoming travel, it's time to buy your ticket. Follow websites prompts for information like your name, number of travelers, frequent flyer number, seat and meal preferences, and credit card information. You can usually pay baggage fees and select seats during your booking sessions. If you're traveling internationally, you will need a passport.

Step 6: Print booking confirmation and other relevant documents. Make sure to take these documents to the airport with you on the day of your flight. Follow the 24-hour rule - within 24 hours of booking your flight, check the prices one last time. If the fare has decreased, call the airline and rebook the flight at the lower price with no penalty.

Method 2: Booking with an Airline or Travel Agent

Step 1: Outline your tentative travel plans. Just as with online booking, think about where you plan or would like to travel, the dates you would like to go, and if you just want to book flights or a package deal. Make a list of your plans and have them available while you talk to the travel or airline agent.

Step 2: Contact a travel agent or an airline representative. You can call either traditional travel agents or airline reps to help you find the best flight booking. Give the agent the information on your tentative travel plans and let them know pertinent information including your seat preferences and if you are flexible in your travel dates and airlines.

Step 3: Compare prices from different agents. Call several travel agents and ask them for price quotes. By comparing what different agents offer, you will get the best flight deal. If you find an agent you like but they don't have the best offer, let them know about the lower price you found and see if they can match or better it.

Step 4: Purchase your ticket. Once you decide on the right flight offer for your upcoming travel, it's time to buy your ticket. Call the agent and let them know which flight you'd like to book. Ask questions about extra fees including taxes, baggage fees, and upgrade costs. Ask about cancellation and refund policies.

Step 5: Get a copy of your booking confirmation and other relevant documents. Make sure to take these documents to the airport with you on the day of your flight to avoid any questions or problems with your booking.

Community Q&A

Q: How long before a flight should I show up at the airport? A: It depends on the airport, but the general rule is at least two hours in advance.

Q: How can I book a flight without a credit card? A: You need a credit card to book a flight, unless the airport allows you to pay with cash.

Q: Is it wise to book a flight five months in advance? A: It depends on your particular situation. If you have found a good deal and are certain you will be taking that trip at that specific time, go for it.

Tips

Consider working with a travel agent to book your flight, as they might be able to get tickets at a negotiable rate or discount. Booking agents are there to help, so don't be afraid to ask questions. Try to book your flight directly through the airline instead of a third-party service because there's usually less hassle around your travel. Consider getting flight insurance to get a refund on your airfare if the flight cancels or if there's a problem.

Article Summary

To book a flight online, you can either go directly to a certain airline's website or visit websites that compare all airlines, like Expedia or Kayak. Follow the website's instructions for choosing your date and airport, and consider clicking the My dates are flexible button for a cheaper option.
with: If you're traveling soon, booking a flight is one of the most important parts of finalizing your plans.
Outline your tentative travel plans. Think about where you plan or might like to travel, the dates you would like to go.
Consider being flexible in your plans. The more flexible you are, the more likely you are to get a great deal on your flight.
Compare flight prices from different sites to get the best flight deal.
Follow the 24-hour rule. Within 24 hours of booking your flight, check the prices one last time.
